one_time_description: Prevention retroactive funding received in 2020 with no prior year equivalent
economic_notes: First Nation has 18.32% ownership in Consolidated Craft Brands (medicinal cannabis company) valued at $2,000,000 cost. Day Star Gas Bar & Confectionary Inc. is a wholly owned business enterprise with revenue of $566,412 and net loss of $31,110. Member of Touchwood Agency Specific Claim negotiating settlement re: mismanagement of funds 1920-1924.
notes: Located in Treaty 4 territory, Saskatchewan. Consolidated entity includes Day Star First Nation and Day Star First Nation CMHC Housing Projects. Day Star Gas Bar & Confectionary Inc. is accounted for using modified equity method (1 GBE). Portfolio investment of $2,000,000 in Consolidated Craft Brands (medicinal cannabis, 18.32% ownership) recorded at cost. Restricted cash includes current ISC capital projects ($52,347) and long-term CMHC reserves ($85,001). CMHC replacement reserve is under-funded by $156,897. Peace Hills Trust loan refinanced during year combining prior loans into new $2,264,647 facility. Prevention retroactive funding of $529,491 is a one-time non-recurring item. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + FNIHB + CMHC + TATC = $1,589,050 + $429,830 + $64,937 + $375,580 = $2,459,397; OSR includes Donations, First Nations Trust, CDC, Other, Rental, Interest, and investment earnings. Remuneration schedule not included in extracted text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
